config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before the rights of the individual, because individual rights are what protect people from abuse by governments or majorities. A society that sacrifices basic rights for the "greater good" can easily become unfair and oppressive. From my perspective, the better principle is that society should pursue the common good while respecting strong limits on what it can take from individuals.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu adalah pagar utama melawan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan oleh mayoritas maupun pemerintah, dan tanpa pagar itu, istilah "kepentingan masyarakat" bisa dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Namun, saya juga tidak berpikir hak individu itu absolut tanpa batas; dalam keadaan tertentu seperti keselamatan publik, masyarakat memang bisa meminta pembatasan yang sempit, jelas, dan dapat dipertanggungjawabkan. Jadi menurut saya, prioritas yang benar bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan keseimbangan yang sangat ketat dengan perlindungan kuat terhadap hak dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because that logic can be abused very easily. From my perspective as an Indonesian, I have seen how appeals to "public interest" or "order" can sometimes be used to silence criticism, excuse corruption, or pressure minorities, so I do not trust society-first thinking when it is too broad. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every situation, because public safety and shared obligations do matter. The right balance is to protect basic rights first and only limit them when there is a clear, necessary, and narrowly defined reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu, karena bagi saya itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat hak individu sebagai perlindungan utama terhadap tirani mayoritas dan pemerintah yang terlalu jauh masuk ke hidup warga. Saya juga tidak bilang hak individu harus absolut; pembatasan bisa dibenarkan untuk keselamatan publik, tetapi harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji secara ketat. Jadi, menurut saya bukan “masyarakat duluan,” melainkan hak dasar dulu, lalu kepentingan bersama hanya dibatasi sejauh benar-benar perlu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how “public interest” can be used too easily to pressure dissenters, excuse corruption, or silence minority voices. Society does matter, but once you put it above individual rights by default, you give too much power to governments and majorities to decide whose rights are expendable. I also do not accept the idea that individual rights must be sacrificed whenever leaders claim it is for order or safety; that argument is often abused. The better standard is to protect basic rights first, and only limit them when the need is clear, narrow, and truly unavoidable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak tegas gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah politik kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai terlalu longgar, itu sering berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk mayoritas atau pemerintah menginjak hak orang yang tidak populer. Saya setuju bahwa keselamatan publik kadang menuntut pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan alasan umum untuk mengorbankan kebebasan. Jadi bagi saya, hak dasar harus menjadi titik awal, dan kepentingan masyarakat hanya boleh membatasi sejauh benar-benar perlu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
